statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.DimCustomer (CustomerAlternateKey, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Title, Gender, MaritalStatus, EmailPromotion, DateFirstPurchase) SELECT CAST(c.CustomerID AS NVARCHAR(15)) AS CustomerAlternateKey, p.FirstName, p.MiddleName, p.LastName, p.Title, NULL AS Gender, NULL AS MaritalStatus, p.EmailPromotion, CAST(h.MinOrderDate AS DATE) AS DateFirstPurchase FROM bronze.Customer c JOIN bronze.Person p ON c.PersonID = p.BusinessEntityID OUTER APPLY (SELECT MIN(OrderDate) AS MinOrderDate FROM bronze.SalesOrderHeader sh WHERE sh.CustomerID = c.CustomerID) h

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(c.CustomerID AS NVARCHAR(15)) AS CustomerAlternateKey, p.FirstName, p.MiddleName, p.LastName, p.Title, NULL AS Gender, NULL AS MaritalStatus, p.EmailPromotion, CAST(h.MinOrderDate AS DATE) AS DateFirstPurchase FROM bronze.Customer c JOIN bronze.Person p ON c.PersonID = p.BusinessEntityID OUTER APPLY ( SELECT MIN(OrderDate) AS MinOrderDate FROM bronze.SalesOrderHeader sh WHERE sh.CustomerID = c.CustomerID ) h
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import_customers AS ( SELECT CustomerID, PersonID FROM bronze.Customer ), import_persons AS ( SELECT BusinessEntityID, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Title, EmailPromotion FROM bronze.Person ), import_orders AS ( SELECT CustomerID, OrderDate FROM bronze.SalesOrderHeader ), logical_customer_first_purchase AS ( SELECT CustomerID, MIN(OrderDate) AS MinOrderDate FROM import_orders GROUP BY CustomerID ), logical_customer_person_join AS ( SELECT c.CustomerID, p.FirstName, p.MiddleName, p.LastName, p.Title, p.EmailPromotion, cfp.MinOrderDate FROM import_customers c JOIN import_persons p ON c.PersonID = p.BusinessEntityID LEFT JOIN logical_customer_first_purchase cfp ON c.CustomerID = cfp.CustomerID ), final AS ( SELECT CAST(CustomerID AS NVARCHAR(15)) AS CustomerAlternateKey, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Title, NULL AS Gender, NULL AS MaritalStatus, EmailPromotion, CAST(MinOrderDate AS DATE) AS DateFirstPurchase FROM logical_customer_person_join ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both queries access bronze.Customer, bronze.Person, and bronze.SalesOrderHeader identically.

output_columns.summary: Both produce the exact same nine columns (CustomerAlternateKey, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Title, Gender, MaritalStatus, EmailPromotion, DateFirstPurchase) with matching data types and NULL values in the same positions.

joins.summary: The OUTER APPLY is correctly converted to a LEFT JOIN with explicit CTE-based aggregation. Customer-Person INNER JOIN on PersonID/BusinessEntityID is preserved.

filters.summary: Neither query applies WHERE filters—both retrieve all matching rows.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both compute MIN(OrderDate) per CustomerID with identical granularity. The extracted query's implicit per-customer grouping in the OUTER APPLY is explicitly defined via GROUP BY CustomerID in the refactored version.
